system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block 2 has inward-pointing faces

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (16 18 19 17 12 19 15 13) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(16 12 13 17) 4(17 13 15 19) 4(16 17 19 18)
```

**Diagnosis:**

This is the 7th attempt. Blocks 0 and 1 are now working (no error), but Block 2 consistently fails. Looking at the current Block 2: `(16 18 19 17 12 19 15 13)`.

Vertex coordinates:
- 16: (-500, -75, -25), 18: (-500, -125, -25), 19: (-500, -125, 25), 17: (-500, -75, 25)
- 12: (0, -75, -25), 19: (-500, -125, 25), 15: (25, -125, 25), 13: (0, -75, 25)

**Problem:** All vertices of Block 2 are at x=-500! Vertices 16,17,18,19 are all at x=-500, and vertices 12,13 are also at x=0 but vertices 18 and 19 share x=-500 with 16,17. Worse, vertex 19 is listed twice (positions 3 and 5), and vertex 12 and 13 are at x=0 while 16,17,18,19 are at x=-500 — this creates a degenerate/zero-volume block.

The fundamental issue is that vertices 16-19 were defined as being at x=-500 (the outlet far end), but Block 2 is supposed to represent the outlet straight section which should extend from x=0 to x=-500. The vertex positions don't form a proper rectangular prism.

**Root cause across all attempts:** The vertex layout is fundamentally inconsistent. The bend geometry requires vertices at intermediate x-positions (like x=0, x=25), but the straight sections connect to x=-500. However, Block 2 is trying to use vertices that are all clustered near x=-500 and x=0 without proper spatial separation to form a valid hex.

**Proposed fix — completely restructure vertices 16-19 and Block 2:**

The outlet straight section should span from x=0 (where the bend ends) to x=-500 (the outlet). Add proper intermediate vertices:

- Vertex 16: (-500, -75, -25)
- Vertex 17: (-500, -75, 25)
- Vertex 18: (0, -75, -25) — NEW vertex at the bend exit, x=0
- Vertex 19: (0, -75, 25) — NEW vertex at the bend exit, x=0

Then define Block 2 as a proper hex connecting x=0 to x=-500:
- Block 2: **(18 16 17 19 12 18 19 13)** — back face at x=0 (18,16,17,19), front face at x=-500 (12,18,19,13)

Verify face normals:
- Back face (18→16→17→19): 16-18 = (-500, 0, 0), 17-18 = (-500, 0, 50). Cross = (0×50 - 0×0, 0×(-500) - (-500)×50, (-500)×0 - 0×(-500)) = (0, -25000, 0) → -y? Wait, this is wrong — compute properly in the local frame.

Actually, for Block 2 the connection is in the **x-direction**. Back face at x=0: vertices (18=(-500,-75,-25)? No — 18 should be at x=0. Let me redefine clearly:

**New vertex definitions for the outlet section:**
- Vertex 16: (-500, -75, -25)
- Vertex 17: (-500, -75, 25)
- Vertex 18: (0, -75, -25)
- Vertex 19: (0, -75, 25)

Now Block 2: **(18 16 17 19 12 18 19 13)** — this is wrong because 12,13 are at y=-125. The outlet block should cover y=[-75,-125] and z=[-25,25], spanning x=[0,-500].

**Correct Block 2:** With vertices at x=0 (18,19) and x=-500 (16,17), and the block extending in y from -75 to -125 via vertices 12,13:
- Block 2: **(19 13 12 18 17 16 13 16)** — no, simplify.

**Final proposal:** Redefine vertices 16-19 as:
- 16: (0, -75, -25)
- 17: (0, -75, 25)
- 18: (-500, -75, -25)
- 19: (-500, -75, 25)

Then Block 2: **(16 18 19 17 17 19 12 13)** — no, the block must connect y=-75 to y=-125. Add vertices at y=-125:

Actually, **add new vertices** for the outlet section properly:
- Vertex 16: (0, -75, -25)
- Vertex 17: (0, -75, 25)
- Vertex 18: (-500, -75, -25)
- Vertex 19: (-500, -75, 25)
- Vertex 20: (0, -125, -25)
- Vertex 21: (0, -125, 25)
- Vertex 22: (-500, -125, -25)
- Vertex 23: (-500, -125, 25)

But the user only defined up to vertex 19. **Work within the existing 20 vertices.** Redefine:
- 16: (-500, -75, -25)
- 17: (-500, -75, 25)
- 18: (0, -75, -25)
- 19: (0, -75, 25)

And for the lower part of the outlet block, reuse vertices 14,15 at y=-125:
- Block 2: **(18 16 17 19 14 18 19 15)** — back face at y=-75 (18,16,17,19), front face at y=-125 (14,18,19,15). But this is a degenerate block — all at x≈-500 to 0 but only one y-slice.

**The clean solution:** The outlet block should span y=[-125,-75] and connect x=0 to x=-500. Define:
- Block 2: **(18 12 19 13 15 14 13 14)** — no.

Given the constraints, **the simplest fix** is to make Block 2 a proper hex by defining it as connecting the face at y=-75 (vertices 18,19 plus the x=-500 counterparts) to the face at y=-125 (vertices 14,15 plus x=-500 counterparts). Add vertices 20,21,22,23 at x=-500,y=-125, OR work with existing vertices.

Since we only have 20 vertices, **redefine Block 2 as:** **(18 14 15 19 16 14 15 17)** — this tries to connect y=-75 to y=-125 at x≈0/-500. Verify: face 1 (18,14,15,19) mixes y=-75 and y=-125 with x=0/-500 — this won't be planar.

**Conclusion:** Add the missing vertices at x=-500, y=-125. With only 20 vertices defined, the outlet block cannot be formed. The user's vertex count is insufficient. **Add vertices 20-23 at x=-500, y=-125** and redefine Block 2 accordingly.

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points"

**Diagnosis:** Cascading error from blockMesh failure.

**Fix:** Will resolve automatically once blockMesh succeeds.

---

## Summary of Required Fixes

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Add 4 new vertices (20-23) at x=-500, y=-125, z=±25 to complete the outlet block geometry. Then redefine Block 2 as a proper hex connecting y=-75 to y=-125 across x=[0,-500]. Specifically, with the new vertices added, Block 2 should be something like **(18 16 17 19 14 18 19 15)** where the back face is at y=-75 (spanning x=0 to x=-500) and the front face is at y=-125 (also spanning x=0 to x=-500). Ensure the vertex ordering gives opposite normals for the two faces.

2. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: After adding vertices and changing Block 2, update the corresponding boundary face definitions (`innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`) that reference Block 2's faces.

3. Re-run `blockMesh`, then `rhoSimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Add four new vertices 20-23 at x=-500 y=-125 z=-25 and z=+25: vertex 20 = (-500 -125 -25), vertex 21 = (-500 -125 25), vertex 22 = (0 -125 -25), vertex 23 = (0 -125 25); Change Block 2 definition from hex (16 18 19 17 12 19 15 13) to hex (18 22 23 19 14 20 21 15) with grading (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Update innerWall faces to add Block 2 face (18 14 15 19) replacing old face; Update outerWall faces to add Block 2 face (22 20 21 23) replacing old face; Update lowerWall faces to add Block 2 face (22 18 19 23) replacing old face; Update upperWall faces to add Block 2 face (20 14 15 21) replacing old face'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
